title: heritiera polyandra
intro: Heritiera polyandra, also known as the "Kadupul tree," is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Sri Lanka and India, and is valued for its medicinal properties, including the ability to lower blood pressure and act as an anti-inflammatory agent. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The herbal preparations of Heritiera polyandra include a decoction of the roots, which can be consumed as a tea or used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
